# native_interop_annotation

Provides the `@ExternalVersions` annotation used by [`ffigen`](https://pub.dev/packages/ffigen)
to express iOS/macOS OS-level API availability constraints on generated native
interop bindings.

## Overview

When `ffigen` parses Objective-C or C headers that carry Apple availability
macros — such as `API_AVAILABLE`, `API_DEPRECATED`, or `API_UNAVAILABLE` —
it emits `@ExternalVersions` annotations on the generated Dart declarations.

```dart
// Generated by ffigen from:
// NS_AVAILABLE_IOS(14_0) - (void)doSomething;

@ExternalVersions({
  'ios': ExternalVersion(min: '14.0'),
  'macos': ExternalVersion(min: '11.0'),
})
external void doSomething();
```

The companion package [`native_api_lint`](../native_api_lint) provides an
analyzer plugin that reads these annotations and compares them against the
Flutter project's minimum deployment target, producing lint warnings at
incompatible call sites.

## Annotations

### `@ExternalVersions`

Applied by `ffigen` to generated Dart methods and classes. Contains a map
from lowercase platform identifiers (`'ios'`, `'macos'`, `'tvos'`, etc.) to
[`ExternalVersion`] instances.

### `ExternalVersion`

Describes the availability window for a single platform:

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `min` | Inclusive minimum OS version the API is available on |
| `max` | Exclusive maximum OS version (removed/obsoleted), `null` if still available |
| `deprecationMessage` | Message from `API_DEPRECATED("...", ...)` macro, `null` if not deprecated |

## Usage

This package is **not** intended to be used directly by application developers.
It is a shared contract between:

1. `ffigen` — which **produces** annotations in generated code.
2. `native_api_lint` — which **reads** annotations to produce lint diagnostics.

## See also

- [ffigen package](https://pub.dev/packages/ffigen) — generates Dart bindings from C/ObjC headers
- [native_api_lint package](../native_api_lint) — the companion analyzer plugin
- [GitHub issue #63618](https://github.com/dart-lang/sdk/issues/63618) — original design discussion
